era_context: Late Bronze Age collapse. Egypt under Ramesses III. Shang dynasty approaching its end. Eastern Mediterranean in transition. Assyria weak. Levantine coast being reshaped.

events.title: Ramesses III's reign; continuation of Egyptian rule
events.description: Ramesses III's reign continues. The Egyptian state maintains its administrative and military structures, though resources are increasingly strained. Temple records and administrative papyri document the ongoing organization of the state.
events.certainty_note: Ramesses III's reign is well-documented; the year falls within his reign (c. 1186–1155 BCE).

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The year 1091 BCE is not independently attested in any source.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 1091 BCE is sparse. No events specific to this year are documented. The year is assigned by modern chronological convention.
